violations.text: The floors in the kitchen (at cook/prep line) and in the basement (near ice machine) were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2026

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by (prep table and other items). PIC relocated other items and moved prep table at time of inspection. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with food debris. PIC relocated can opener to dishwasher to be washed, rinsed and sanitized. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

Cole slaw was observed held at (50) degrees Fahrenheit located in 2 door prep unit. Prep unit ambient 39 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC voluntarily disposed of Cole slaw. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

The presence of (pest) is not controlled as evidenced by (gnaw marks observed on a bag of rice flour). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Establishment must contact a licensed pest control service to conduct a full assessment/evaluation of facility. Pest control service must provide a plan of action detailing treatment plans and a target date to eliminate pests. Plan of action must be emailed to inspector before the re-inspection date. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2025
The dry storage in the (basement) area has an accumulation of pest droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025
The floors in the kitchen (at cook/prep line) and in the basement (near ice machine) were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025
The following equipment were observed in poor repair: 1) The inner plate on the door of the chest freezer was loose Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
The floors in the kitchen (at cook/prep line) and in the basement (near ice machine) were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The hand wash sink at the bar was observed being used as a dump sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
White chowder was observed held past its seven day date marking. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Doors to the outside, located in the basement area and side dining room area, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The following equipment were observed in poor repair: 1) A knife was heavily chipped (PIC instructed to remove knife from use) 2) The inner plate on the door of the chest freezer in the kitchen was loose 3) The gaskets of a refrigeration unit were loose 4) The inner plate on the door of a refrigeration unit was loose New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine and quaternary ammonium sanitizing solutions for the dish machine, three bay sink, and quat dispenser at mop sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
The area above the cook line (ansul system, etc.) had an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The ice machine is lacking a proper air gap (air break observed). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
The floors in the kitchen (at cook/prep line) and in the basement (near ice machine) were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

The floors throughout the basement storage areas have an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2024
The handwash sink in the bar area was being used as a dump sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023

Food contact surfaces of (deli slicer) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Proper cleaning procedures reviewed with PIC. Slicer to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023

ROP crab was observed thawing with package in tact. ROP seafood packaging must be sliced open or product must be removed from package prior to thawing. ROP crab was observed @ 37 degrees and was sliced open. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of bio-film. Inspector recommends increasing routine cleaning frequency to prevent an accumulation of bio-film. Ice machine to be emptied and washed, rinsed, and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023
The floors in the kitchen area/near the door to the outside is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2023
